rulings.Case Title: KATY CHMURA VS. LAWRENCE CHOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Christina Sutton'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/ Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014 line 9. Defendant Christina Sutton'S Demurrer To Complaint Sustained in part and overruled in part. As a preliminary matter, demurrer as to all causes of action based on lack of landlord-tenant relationship is overruled. Demurrer to Negligence cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege harm caused by alleged breach of duty. No substantive opposition on this point. Demurrer to Breach of Warranty of Habitability is sustained with leave to amend to allege substantial defects in the rental unit and substantial failures to comply with codes. No substantive opposition on this point. Demurrer to the Breach of Warranty of Quiet Enjoyment is overruled. This cause of action is sufficiently alleged. See SFRO 37.10B(a)(10). Demurrer to Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege all elements of this cause of action. Paragraph 74 is insufficient. Demurer to Fraud cause of action should be sustained with leave to amend to allege fraud with requisite specificity. Paragraphs 81-82 are insufficient. Demurrer to cause of action for Constructive Eviction is overruled. Complaint sufficiently alleges Sutton's duties and responsibilities as a property manager (Ex. A). Whether Sutton is a "decision maker for the property, has a say in how property is maintained, how repairs are effectuated, or haw landlord-tenant issues are handled" (Demurer 8:4-6) are issues of fact not properly raised on demurrer. Moving party and her counsel are order to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ VS. ESTELA MARTINEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/ Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014 line 6. DEFENDANT ESTELA MARTINEZ's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Defendant's motion does not dispose of all the issues in this case, i.e. First Cause of action based on Three Day Notice to Cure or Quit is not addressed, specifically non-payment of pass-through charges. Defendant does not seek summary adjudication. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN S. DUPEN VS. MICHAEL CLANCY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/ Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 18, 2014 line 7. DEFENDANT MICHAEL CLANCY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled, 5 days to answer. Non-compliance with SFRO rent increase limitations should be raised as an affirmative defense. Nourafchan v. Miner (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 746. = (501/RBU)
